title: verbascum boerhavii f. bicolor
intro: Verbascum boerhavii f. bicolor, also known as the common mullein or mullein, is a biennial plant belonging to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). It is a perennial flowering plant with large, hairy leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, and has been used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Mullein can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or ointment, and has also been used to create herbal salves and creams to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
